nativeRange.habitat: Banks of slow-moving streams and rivers in Sri Lanka, often in shaded, moderately hard water. Like other Sri Lankan Crypts, naturally found rooted in soft sediment along watercourses.

avoidWith: Silver Dollars and other plant-eating fish

summary: A compact Sri Lankan Cryptocoryne, generally considered beginner-friendly, with the small reddish 'Petchii' trade variant widely sold. Forms tidy rosettes suitable for the midground of planted tanks.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne beckettii is a medium-sized rosette Cryptocoryne native to Sri Lanka, where it grows along the banks of slow-moving streams and rivers. It is one of the more popular Crypts in the planted-tank hobby and is generally regarded as beginner-friendly once established. Mature height is typically in the 10-20 cm range; the widely sold 'Petchii' trade variant is a smaller, more compact form (commonly 10-15 cm) with narrower, slightly fluted leaves that often show olive-brown to reddish-brown upper surfaces and violet/red undersides depending on lighting and nutrient conditions. Some sources market 'Petchii' as a separate species (Cryptocoryne petchii), but it is generally treated in the hobby as a cultivar/variant of C. beckettii. Lighting requirements are flexible — low to moderate light is generally sufficient, with higher light tending to bring out the reddish-brown coloration but also increasing algae pressure. CO2 is not strictly required but has been reported to improve growth rate, leaf size, and color saturation. Cryptocoryne beckettii is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER — nutrient uptake happens primarily through the root zone, so a nutrient-rich substrate (aqua soil) and/or regular root tabs near the root mass are strongly recommended. Plant by burying the roots up to the crown without burying the crown itself, which can rot. CRYPT MELT WARNING: when newly planted or after a significant water-parameter change, this species frequently sheds most or all of its existing leaves in a process called 'crypt melt'. This is normal — the rhizome and roots typically push out new leaves adapted to the new conditions within 2-4 weeks, so the plant should be left in place during a melt event. To minimize melt, avoid relocating an established plant. Temperature consensus across sources sits at roughly 22-28 °C, with Aquasabi reporting a wider tolerance of 15-30 °C; pH consensus is 6.0-7.5 with a tolerance band of 5-8; gH and kH ranges are forgiving, with the plant doing well in moderately hard water. Source disagreement note: Buce Plant lists native range as 'South Asia' (broad but consistent) and a smaller 4-6 inch height; Tropica, Aquasabi, aqua-fish.net, and Modern Aquarium all confirm Sri Lanka and a 10-20 cm range. This entry covers the species at the C. beckettii level and consolidates the 'Petchii' trade variant under alsoKnownAs.
breedingNotes: Propagated primarily by runners (stolons) that produce daughter plantlets along a horizontal rhizome; once a daughter has developed several leaves and a healthy root system, it can be cut from the runner and replanted. Rhizome division is also possible — cut a healthy rhizome into sections each retaining leaves and roots. Cryptocoryne beckettii can also flower (producing the spathe-and-spadix inflorescence typical of Araceae) when grown emersed, but seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica product page for Cryptocoryne beckettii 'Petchii' (108A). Confirmed Sri Lankan origin, 10-15 cm height for the Petchii variant, low light demand, low CO2 demand, medium growth rate, easy difficulty, and foreground-to-midground placement. The Tropica search-page URL supplied in the manifest redirected to this product page, which was used as the canonical primary source.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Cross-checked Sri Lankan origin, low-to-moderate lighting, CO2 not required, 22-28 °C (72-82 °F), slow-to-moderate growth, foreground-to-midground placement, nutrient-rich substrate / root tabs recommendation, and the crypt-melt acclimation warning.

additional.name: Aquasabi
additional.notes: Authoritative parameter source: temperature 15-30 °C, pH 5-8, gH 0-30 °dGH, kH 1-18 °dKH, height 10-20 cm, medium growth, midground placement, runners + rhizome division for propagation, and confirmation of Petchii as a triploid variant with brown upper / red lower leaf surfaces.

additional.name: aqua-fish.net
additional.notes: Cross-checked Sri Lankan native range, adult size 15-20 cm, 23-28 °C, pH 6.8-7.5, gH 4-18 °dGH, medium light, easy difficulty, midground placement, and runner-based propagation.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked low-to-moderate light, CO2 recommended, slow growth, midground placement, nutrient-rich substrate, runner propagation, and crypt-melt warning. Buce Plant states a smaller 4-6 inch (10-15 cm) height range and 'South Asia' native range; the 10-20 cm range and Sri Lanka country tag from Tropica, Aquasabi, and aqua-fish.net were preferred.
